transcript: Speaker A: 2 ಕಪ್ ಚಹಾ ಎಷ್ಟು? [How much for 2 cups of tea?] Speaker B: That would be 24 rupees, since each cup of tea is 12 rupees. Speaker A: ಓ ಹಾಗಾ, UPI ತಗೋತೀರಾ? [Oh I see, do you accept UPI?] Speaker B: Yeah sure, you can pay using UPI. Speaker A: ನಿಮ್ಮ English ತುಂಬಾ ಚೆನ್ನಾಗಿದೆ, YouTube ನೋಡಿ ಕಲಿತೀರಾ? [Your English is very good, did you learn from YouTube?] Speaker B: ಇಲ್ಲ, English ಕಲಿಬೇಕಂದ್ರೆ practice ಮಾಡ್ಬೇಕು, YouTube ನಲ್ಲಿ practice ಹೇಗೆ ಮಾಡೋದು? [No, to learn English you need to practice, how do you practice on YouTube?] Speaker A: ಹೌದು, ನೀವು ಹೇಳೋದು ಸರಿ, ಹಾಗಿದ್ರೆ ಎಲ್ಲಿ ಕಲಿತೀರಿ? [Yes, what you say is right, so where do you learn?] Speaker B: MySivi ಅನ್ನೋ ಒಂದು app ಇದೆ, ಅದರಲ್ಲಿ ಒಬ್ಬ AI teacher ಇದ್ದಾರೆ, ಅವರ ಜೊತೆ practice ಮಾಡಿ ನಾನು English ಕಲಿತಾ ಇದ್ದೀನಿ. [There is an app called MySivi, there is an AI teacher in it, I am practicing with them and learning English.] Speaker A: ಓ ಹಾಗಾ, ಆದರೆ ಈ app ತುಂಬಾ costly ಇರಬಹುದು ಅಲ್ವಾ? [Oh I see, but this app must be very costly, right?] Speaker B: ಇಲ್ಲ ಇಲ್ಲ, ನಾನು ಕೇವಲ 1 rupee ಇಂದಲೇ start ಮಾಡಿದ್ದೀನಿ, ಎಲ್ಲಕ್ಕಿಂತ best part ಏನಂದ್ರೆ, ಇಲ್ಲಿ ನಾವು ತಪ್ಪು English ಮಾತಾಡಿದ್ರು ಯಾರೂ judge ಮಾಡೋದಿಲ್ಲ, ಕೇವಲ ನಮ್ಮ mistakes ಅನ್ನ correct ಮಾಡ್ತಾರೆ. [No no, I started with just 1 rupee, and the best part is, even if we speak wrong English here, no one judges us, they only correct our mistakes.] Speaker A: ಅರೆ ವಾಹ್, ಹಾಗಿದ್ರೆ ಎಲ್ಲರೂ ಈ app use ಮಾಡ್ಬೇಕು. [Oh wow, then everyone should use this app.] Speaker B: ಖಂಡಿತವಾಗಿ. [Definitely.] Speaker C: Click on install now and download MySivi app.

summary: A train passenger is impressed by a tea seller's fluent English. The tea seller explains he practices with an AI teacher on the MySivi app, which costs only 1 rupee and corrects mistakes without judgment. The ad ends with a call to install the app.
